summary: Patched the measurement-hold release scheduler so it no longer preserves a same-time cron job with stale re-entry instructions. The scheduler now inspects the existing job payload, replaces stale matching jobs, and re-schedules the post-hold run with the full required marketing context.
why_now: The active hold window already had a scheduled post-hold rerun at 2026-05-28T09:12:15.
That rerun was missing the full required context block for this marketing loop, which reduced the odds of a truthful executable lane after hold release.
Improving the scheduled rerun's payload was the highest-leverage concrete process repair available during the hold window without regenerating fake progress packets.
shared_findings_reused: distribution_lane_latest.json: short review window remains active until 2026-05-28T09:12:15
outcome_execution_board_latest.json: no truthful do-now packet exists during the current hold
post_hold_distribution_reentry_latest.md: first post-hold slot must choose a real lane or do a concrete repair
measurement_hold_release_cron.json: a post-hold rerun already existed and should be improved rather than duplicated

behavior: Build the post-hold rerun message from a single helper that includes the required context file list and freshest artifact list.
Inspect matching cron jobs with openclaw cron show before treating them as already scheduled.
Replace a same-time scheduled job when its payload message is stale instead of silently preserving it.
